"""
QuestHub - Quest management, skill planning, and real-time collaboration for RPG players.

This is a single-file, dependency-free (standard library only) implementation that
demonstrates a full-stack application architecture:

    Database   -> thread-safe SQLite repositories (users, quests, skills, collaboration)
    Backend    -> authentication, quest/skill services, real-time collaboration engine
    Integration-> in-process service facade + an HTTP REST API + a pub/sub event bus
    Frontend   -> a programmatic client that reacts to live collaboration events

The code is organised into logical modules. Because the deliverable must be a single
file, each module is delimited by a `# <module>.py` banner comment.

Run it directly:

    python solution.py            # run the self-test suite, then a narrative demo
    python solution.py --test     # run only the self-test suite
    python solution.py --demo     # run only the narrative demo
    python solution.py --serve    # start the HTTP API server (Ctrl+C to stop)
"""

from __future__ import annotations


# ======================================================================================
# models.py
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain models and value objects. Plain dataclasses keep the data layer transport- and
# storage-agnostic; every model exposes ``to_dict`` for JSON/API serialization.
# ======================================================================================

import enum
import time
import uuid
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional


def generate_id(prefix: str = "") -> str:
    """Return a collision-resistant identifier with an optional human-readable prefix."""
    return f"{prefix}{uuid.uuid4().hex}"


def now_ts() -> float:
    """Current time as a UTC epoch timestamp (seconds)."""
    return time.time()


def iso(ts: Optional[float]) -> Optional[str]:
    """Convert an epoch timestamp into an ISO-8601 UTC string."""
    if ts is None:
        return None
    return datetime.fromtimestamp(ts, tz=timezone.utc).isoformat()


class QuestStatus(enum.Enum):
    DRAFT = "draft"
    ACTIVE = "active"
    IN_PROGRESS = "in_progress"
    COMPLETED = "completed"
    ABANDONED = "abandoned"


class QuestDifficulty(enum.Enum):
    TRIVIAL = 1
    EASY = 2
    NORMAL = 3
    HARD = 4
    EPIC = 5
    LEGENDARY = 6


class SkillCategory(enum.Enum):
    COMBAT = "combat"
    MAGIC = "magic"
    CRAFTING = "crafting"
    SOCIAL = "social"
    EXPLORATION = "exploration"
    STEALTH = "stealth"


class CollaboratorRole(enum.Enum):
    OWNER = "owner"
    EDITOR = "editor"
    VIEWER = "viewer"


@dataclass
class User:
    id: str
    username: str
    email: str
    display_name: str
    level: int = 1
    xp: int = 0
    created_at: float = field(default_factory=now_ts)

    def to_dict(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        return {
            "id": self.id,
            "username": self.username,
            "email": self.email,
            "display_name": self.display_name,
            "level": self.level,
            "xp": self.xp,
            "created_at": iso(self.created_at),
        }


@dataclass
class Objective:
    id: str
    quest_id: str
    description: str
    completed: bool = False
    order_index: int = 0

    def to_dict(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        return {
            "id": self.id,
            "quest_id": self.quest_id,
            "description": self.description,
            "completed": self.completed,
            "order_index": self.order_index,
        }


@dataclass
class Quest:
    id: str
    title: str
    description: str
    owner_id: str
    status: QuestStatus = QuestStatus.DRAFT
    difficulty: QuestDifficulty = QuestDifficulty.NORMAL
    assignee_id: Optional[str] = None
    xp_reward: int = 100
    version: int = 1
    objectives: List[Objective] = field(default_factory=list)
    created_at: float = field(default_factory=now_ts)
    updated_at: float = field(default_factory=now_ts)

    def progress(self) -> float:
        if not self.objectives:
            return 1.0 if self.status == QuestStatus.COMPLETED else 0.0
        done = sum(1 for o in self.objectives if o.completed)
        return done / len(self.objectives)

    def to_dict(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        return {
            "id": self.id,
            "title": self.title,
            "description": self.description,
            "owner_id": self.owner_id,
            "status": self.status.value,
            "difficulty": self.difficulty.value,
            "assignee_id": self.assignee_id,
            "xp_reward": self.xp_reward,
            "version": self.version,
            "progress": round(self.progress(), 3),
            "objectives": [o.to_dict() for o in self.objectives],
            "created_at": iso(self.created_at),
            "updated_at": iso(self.updated_at),
        }


@dataclass
class Skill:
    id: str
    plan_id: str
    name: str
    category: SkillCategory
    rank: int = 0
    max_rank: int = 5
    cost: int = 1
    prerequisites: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    order_index: int = 0

    def to_dict(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        return {
            "id": self.id,
            "plan_id": self.plan_id,
            "name": self.name,
            "category": self.category.value,
            "rank": self.rank,
            "max_rank": self.max_rank,
            "cost": self.cost,
            "prerequisites": list(self.prerequisites),
            "order_index": self.order_index,
        }


@dataclass
class SkillPlan:
    id: str
    name: str
    owner_id: str
    character_name: str
    description: str = ""
    total_points: int = 20
    version: int = 1
    skills: List[Skill] = field(default_factory=list)
    created_at: float = field(default_factory=now_ts)
    updated_at: float = field(default_factory=now_ts)

    def spent_points(self) -> int:
        return sum(s.rank * s.cost for s in self.skills)

    def remaining_points(self) -> int:
        return self.total_points - self.spent_points()

    def to_dict(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        return {
            "id": self.id,
            "name": self.name,
            "owner_id": self.owner_id,
            "character_name": self.character_name,
            "description": self.description,
            "total_points": self.total_points,
            "spent_points": self.spent_points(),
            "remaining_points": self.remaining_points(),
            "version": self.version,
            "skills": [s.to_dict() for s in self.skills],
            "created_at": iso(self.created_at),
            "updated_at": iso(self.updated_at),
        }


@dataclass
class CollaborationSession:
    id: str
    resource_type: str          # "quest" | "skillplan"
    resource_id: str
    owner_id: str
    active: bool = True
    participants: Dict[str, CollaboratorRole] = field(default_factory=dict)
    created_at: float = field(default_factory=now_ts)

    def to_dict(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        return {
            "id": self.id,
            "resource_type": self.resource_type,
            "resource_id": self.resource_id,
            "owner_id": self.owner_id,
            "active": self.active,
            "participants": {uid: role.value for uid, role in self.participants.items()},
            "created_at": iso(self.created_at),
        }


# ======================================================================================
# errors.py
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Typed exceptions so the API/integration layer can translate failures into meaningful
# status codes instead of leaking stack traces.
# ======================================================================================


class QuestHubError(Exception):
    """Base error. ``status`` maps onto an HTTP status code in the API layer."""
    status = 400


class ValidationError(QuestHubError):
    status = 422


class NotFoundError(QuestHubError):
    status = 404


class AuthError(QuestHubError):
    status = 401


class PermissionError_(QuestHubError):
    status = 403


class ConflictError(QuestHubError):
    """Raised when an optimistic-concurrency version check fails."""
    status = 409


# ======================================================================================
# database.py
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# A thread-safe persistence layer built on SQLite. A single shared connection is guarded
# by a re-entrant lock so background collaboration threads and the HTTP server can share
# one consistent in-memory (or on-disk) database. Repositories translate rows <-> models.
# ======================================================================================

import json
import sqlite3
import threading


SCHEMA = """
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (
    id            TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    username      TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    email         TEXT NOT NULL,
    display_name  TEXT NOT NULL,
    password_salt TEXT NOT NULL,
    password_hash TEXT NOT NULL,
    level         INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
    xp            INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    created_at    REAL NOT NULL
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS auth_sessions (
    token      TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    user_id    TEXT NOT NULL,
    created_at REAL NOT NULL,
    expires_at REAL NOT NULL
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS quests (
    id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    title       TEXT NOT NULL,
    description TEXT NOT NULL,
    owner_id    TEXT NOT NULL,
    status      TEXT NOT NULL,
    difficulty  INTEGER NOT NULL,
    assignee_id TEXT,
    xp_reward   INTEGER NOT NULL,
    version     INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
    created_at  REAL NOT NULL,
    updated_at  REAL NOT NULL
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS objectives (
    id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    quest_id    TEXT NOT NULL,
    description TEXT NOT NULL,
    completed   INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    order_index INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS skill_plans (
    id             TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    name           TEXT NOT NULL,
    owner_id       TEXT NOT NULL,
    character_name TEXT NOT NULL,
    description    TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
    total_points   INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 20,
    version        INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
    created_at     REAL NOT NULL,
    updated_at     REAL NOT NULL
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS skills (
    id            TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    plan_id       TEXT NOT NULL,
    name          TEXT NOT NULL,
    category      TEXT NOT NULL,
    rank          INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    max_rank      INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 5,
    cost          INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
    prerequisites TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]',
    order_index   INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS collab_sessions (
    id            TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    resource_type TEXT NOT NULL,
    resource_id   TEXT NOT NULL,
    owner_id      TEXT NOT NULL,
    active        INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
    created_at    REAL NOT NULL
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS collab_participants (
    session_id TEXT NOT NULL,
    user_id    TEXT NOT NULL,
    role       TEXT NOT NULL,
    joined_at  REAL NOT NULL,
    PRIMARY KEY (session_id, user_id)
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS activity_log (
    id            TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    actor_id      TEXT,
    action        TEXT NOT NULL,
    resource_type TEXT,
    resource_id   TEXT,
    detail        TEXT,
    created_at    REAL NOT NULL
);

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_quests_owner ON quests(owner_id);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_objectives_quest ON objectives(quest_id);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_skills_plan ON skills(plan_id);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_participants_session ON collab_participants(session_id);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_activity_resource ON activity_log(resource_type, resource_id);
"""


class Database:
    """Owns the SQLite connection and serialises all access through a re-entrant lock."""

    def __init__(self, path: str = ":memory:"):
        # check_same_thread=False is safe because every access is wrapped by self.lock.
        self._conn = sqlite3.connect(path, check_same_thread=False)
        self._conn.row_factory = sqlite3.Row
        self._conn.execute("PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON")
        self.lock = threading.RLock()
        with self.lock:
            self._conn.executescript(SCHEMA)
            self._conn.commit()

    def execute(self, sql: str, params: tuple = ()) -> sqlite3.Cursor:
        with self.lock:
            cur = self._conn.execute(sql, params)
            self._conn.commit()
            return cur

    def query_one(self, sql: str, params: tuple = ()) -> Optional[sqlite3.Row]:
        with self.lock:
            cur = self._conn.execute(sql, params)
            return cur.fetchone()

    def query_all(self, sql: str, params: tuple = ()) -> List[sqlite3.Row]:
        with self.lock:
            cur = self._conn.execute(sql, params)
            return cur.fetchall()

    def close(self) -> None:
        with self.lock:
            self._conn.close()


class UserRepository:
    def __init__(self, db: Database):
        self.db = db

    def create(self, user: User, salt: str, pwd_hash: str) -> None:
        self.db.execute(
            "INSERT INTO users (id, username, email, display_name, password_salt, "
            "password_hash, level, xp, created_at) VALUES (?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?)",
            (user.id, user.username, user.email, user.display_name, salt, pwd_hash,
             user.level, user.xp, user.created_at),
        )

    def _row_to_user(self, row: sqlite3.Row) -> User:
        return User(
            id=row["id"], username=row["username"], email=row["email"],
            display_name=row["display_name"], level=row["level"], xp=row["xp"],
            created_at=row["created_at"],
        )

    def by_username(self, username: str) -> Optional[sqlite3.Row]:
        return self.db.query_one("SELECT * FROM users WHERE username = ?", (username,))

    def by_id(self, user_id: str) -> Optional[User]:
        row = self.db.query_one("SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ?", (user_id,))
        return self._row_to_user(row) if row else None

    def add_xp(self, user_id: str, amount: int) -> None:
        with self.db.lock:
            row = self.db.query_one("SELECT level, xp FROM users WHERE id = ?", (user_id,))
            if not row:
                return
            xp = row["xp"] + amount
            level = 1 + xp // 1000  # simple progression: 1000 xp per level
            self.db.execute("UPDATE users SET xp = ?, level = ? WHERE id = ?",
                            (xp, level, user_id))


class AuthSessionRepository:
    def __init__(self, db: Database):
        self.db = db

    def create(self, token: str, user_id: str, ttl_seconds: int) -> None:
        created = now_ts()
        self.db.execute(
            "INSERT INTO auth_sessions (token, user_id, created_at, expires_at) VALUES (?,?,?,?)",
            (token, user_id, created, created + ttl_seconds),
        )

    def get(self, token: str) -> Optional[sqlite3.Row]:
        return self.db.query_one("SELECT * FROM auth_sessions WHERE token = ?", (token,))

    def delete(self, token: str) -> None:
        self.db.execute("DELETE FROM auth_sessions WHERE token = ?", (token,))


class QuestRepository:
    def __init__(self, db: Database):
        self.db = db

    def save(self, quest: Quest) -> None:
        self.db.execute(
            "INSERT OR REPLACE INTO quests (id, title, description, owner_id, status, "
            "difficulty, assignee_id, xp_reward, version, created_at, updated_at) "
            "VALUES (?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?)",
            (quest.id, quest.title, quest.description, quest.owner_id, quest.status.value,
             quest.difficulty.value, quest.assignee_id, quest.xp_reward, quest.version,
             quest.created_at, quest.updated_at),
        )

    def save_objective(self, obj: Objective) -> None:
        self.db.execute(
            "INSERT OR REPLACE INTO objectives (id, quest_id, description, completed, "
            "order_index) VALUES (?,?,?,?,?)",
            (obj.id, obj.quest_id, obj.description, int(obj.completed), obj.order_index),
        )

    def _load_objectives(self, quest_id: str) -> List[Objective]:
        rows = self.db.query_all(
            "SELECT * FROM objectives WHERE quest_id = ? ORDER BY order_index", (quest_id,))
        return [Objective(id=r["id"], quest_id=r["quest_id"], description=r["description"],
                          completed=bool(r["completed"]), order_index=r["order_index"])
                for r in rows]

    def _row_to_quest(self, row: sqlite3.Row) -> Quest:
        quest = Quest(
            id=row["id"], title=row["title"], description=row["description"],
            owner_id=row["owner_id"], status=QuestStatus(row["status"]),
            difficulty=QuestDifficulty(row["difficulty"]), assignee_id=row["assignee_id"],
            xp_reward=row["xp_reward"], version=row["version"],
            created_at=row["created_at"], updated_at=row["updated_at"],
        )
        quest.objectives = self._load_objectives(quest.id)
        return quest

    def by_id(self, quest_id: str) -> Optional[Quest]:
        row = self.db.query_one("SELECT * FROM quests WHERE id = ?", (quest_id,))
        return self._row_to_quest(row) if row else None

    def list_for_user(self, user_id: str) -> List[Quest]:
        rows = self.db.query_all(
            "SELECT * FROM quests WHERE owner_id = ? OR assignee_id = ? ORDER BY created_at DESC",
            (user_id, user_id))
        return [self._row_to_quest(r) for r in rows]

    def delete(self, quest_id: str) -> None:
        self.db.execute("DELETE FROM objectives WHERE quest_id = ?", (quest_id,))
        self.db.execute("DELETE FROM quests WHERE id = ?", (quest_id,))


class SkillPlanRepository:
    def __init__(self, db: Database):
        self.db = db

    def save(self, plan: SkillPlan) -> None:
        self.db.execute(
            "INSERT OR REPLACE INTO skill_plans (id, name, owner_id, character_name, "
            "description, total_points, version, created_at, updated_at) VALUES (?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?)",
            (plan.id, plan.name, plan.owner_id, plan.character_name, plan.description,
             plan.total_points, plan.version, plan.created_at, plan.updated_at),
        )

    def save_skill(self, skill: Skill) -> None:
        self.db.execute(
            "INSERT OR REPLACE INTO skills (id, plan_id, name, category, rank, max_rank, "
            "cost, prerequisites, order_index) VALUES (?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?)",
            (skill.id, skill.plan_id, skill.name, skill.category.value, skill.rank,
             skill.max_rank, skill.cost, json.dumps(skill.prerequisites), skill.order_index),
        )

    def _load_skills(self, plan_id: str) -> List[Skill]:
        rows = self.db.query_all(
            "SELECT * FROM skills WHERE plan_id = ? ORDER BY order_index", (plan_id,))
        return [Skill(id=r["id"], plan_id=r["plan_id"], name=r["name"],
                      category=SkillCategory(r["category"]), rank=r["rank"],
                      max_rank=r["max_rank"], cost=r["cost"],
                      prerequisites=json.loads(r["prerequisites"]), order_index=r["order_index"])
                for r in rows]

    def _row_to_plan(self, row: sqlite3.Row) -> SkillPlan:
        plan = SkillPlan(
            id=row["id"], name=row["name"], owner_id=row["owner_id"],
            character_name=row["character_name"], description=row["description"],
            total_points=row["total_points"], version=row["version"],
            created_at=row["created_at"], updated_at=row["updated_at"],
        )
        plan.skills = self._load_skills(plan.id)
        return plan

    def by_id(self, plan_id: str) -> Optional[SkillPlan]:
        row = self.db.query_one("SELECT * FROM skill_plans WHERE id = ?", (plan_id,))
        return self._row_to_plan(row) if row else None

    def list_for_user(self, user_id: str) -> List[SkillPlan]:
        rows = self.db.query_all(
            "SELECT * FROM skill_plans WHERE owner_id = ? ORDER BY created_at DESC", (user_id,))
        return [self._row_to_plan(r) for r in rows]


class CollaborationRepository:
    def __init__(self, db: Database):
        self.db = db

    def save_session(self, s: CollaborationSession) -> None:
        self.db.execute(
            "INSERT OR REPLACE INTO collab_sessions (id, resource_type, resource_id, "
            "owner_id, active, created_at) VALUES (?,?,?,?,?,?)",
            (s.id, s.resource_type, s.resource_id, s.owner_id, int(s.active), s.created_at),
        )

    def add_participant(self, session_id: str, user_id: str, role: CollaboratorRole) -> None:
        self.db.execute(
            "INSERT OR REPLACE INTO collab_participants (session_id, user_id, role, joined_at) "
            "VALUES (?,?,?,?)",
            (session_id, user_id, role.value, now_ts()),
        )

    def remove_participant(self, session_id: str, user_id: str) -> None:
        self.db.execute(
            "DELETE FROM collab_participants WHERE session_id = ? AND user_id = ?",
            (session_id, user_id))

    def by_id(self, session_id: str) -> Optional[CollaborationSession]:
        row = self.db.query_one("SELECT * FROM collab_sessions WHERE id = ?", (session_id,))
        if not row:
            return None
        s = CollaborationSession(
            id=row["id"], resource_type=row["resource_type"], resource_id=row["resource_id"],
            owner_id=row["owner_id"], active=bool(row["active"]), created_at=row["created_at"])
        for p in self.db.query_all(
                "SELECT * FROM collab_participants WHERE session_id = ?", (session_id,)):
            s.participants[p["user_id"]] = CollaboratorRole(p["role"])
        return s

    def find_for_resource(self, resource_type: str, resource_id: str
                          ) -> Optional[CollaborationSession]:
        row = self.db.query_one(
            "SELECT id FROM collab_sessions WHERE resource_type = ? AND resource_id = ? "
            "AND active = 1", (resource_type, resource_id))
        return self.by_id(row["id"]) if row else None


class ActivityRepository:
    def __init__(self, db: Database):
        self.db = db

    def log(self, actor_id: Optional[str], action: str, resource_type: str = None,
            resource_id: str = None, detail: Dict[str, Any] = None) -> None:
        self.db.execute(
            "INSERT INTO activity_log (id, actor_id, action, resource_type, resource_id, "
            "detail, created_at) VALUES (?,?,?,?,?,?,?)",
            (generate_id("act_"), actor_id, action, resource_type, resource_id,
             json.dumps(detail or {}), now_ts()),
        )

    def recent(self, resource_type: str, resource_id: str, limit: int = 20) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
        rows = self.db.query_all(
            "SELECT * FROM activity_log WHERE resource_type = ? AND resource_id = ? "
            "ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT ?", (resource_type, resource_id, limit))
        return [{"actor_id": r["actor_id"], "action": r["action"],
                 "detail": json.loads(r["detail"]), "at": iso(r["created_at"])} for r in rows]


# ======================================================================================
# event_bus.py
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# The backbone of real-time collaboration: a thread-safe, topic-based publish/subscribe
# bus with an asynchronous dispatch worker. Services publish domain events; client
# connections subscribe to resource topics and receive live updates. This is the
# in-process analogue of a WebSocket fan-out hub.
# ======================================================================================

import queue


@dataclass
class Event:
    type: str
    topic: str
    payload: Dict[str, Any]
    timestamp: float = field(default_factory=now_ts)
    id: str = field(default_factory=lambda: generate_id("evt_"))

    def to_dict(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        return {"id": self.id, "type": self.type, "topic": self.topic,
                "payload": self.payload, "timestamp": iso(self.timestamp)}


class EventBus:
    """Asynchronous topic pub/sub. Subscribe to a concrete topic or to '*' for all events."""

    def __init__(self):
        self._subs: Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]] = {}
        self._lock = threading.RLock()
        self._queue: "queue.Queue" = queue.Queue()
        self._running = True
        self._worker = threading.Thread(
            target=self._dispatch_loop, name="EventBusDispatcher", daemon=True)
        self._worker.start()

    def subscribe(self, topic: str, callback) -> str:
        sub_id = generate_id("sub_")
        with self._lock:
            self._subs.setdefault(topic, {})[sub_id] = callback
        return sub_id

    def unsubscribe(self, sub_id: str) -> bool:
        with self._lock:
            for topic, subs in list(self._subs.items()):
                if sub_id in subs:
                    del subs[sub_id]
                    if not subs:
                        del self._subs[topic]
                    return True
        return False

    def publish(self, event: Event) -> None:
        self._queue.put(event)

    def _dispatch_loop(self) -> None:
        while self._running:
            try:
                event = self._queue.get(timeout=0.1)
            except queue.Empty:
                continue
            with self._lock:
                callbacks = list(self._subs.get(event.topic, {}).values())
                callbacks += list(self._subs.get("*", {}).values())
            for cb in callbacks:
                try:
                    cb(event)
                except Exception:
                    # A misbehaving subscriber must never take down the dispatcher.
                    pass

    def drain(self, timeout: float = 1.0) -> None:
        """Block until all currently-queued events have been dispatched (used by tests)."""
        deadline = time.time() + timeout
        while time.time() < deadline:
            if self._queue.unfinished_tasks == 0 and self._queue.empty():
                # Give the worker a moment to finish in-flight callbacks.
                time.sleep(0.02)
                if self._queue.empty():
                    return
            time.sleep(0.01)

    def stop(self) -> None:
        self._running = False


class RealtimeConnection:
    """
    Represents a single connected client device/session. It buffers inbound events in an
    inbox queue (like a WebSocket frame buffer) and can invoke an optional push callback
    so a frontend can react immediately to collaboration updates.
    """

    def __init__(self, user_id: str, bus: EventBus, on_message=None):
        self.id = generate_id("conn_")
        self.user_id = user_id
        self._bus = bus
        self.inbox: "queue.Queue" = queue.Queue()
        self.on_message = on_message
        self._subs: Dict[str, str] = {}  # topic -> subscription id

    def subscribe(self, topic: str) -> None:
        if topic in self._subs:
            return
        self._subs[topic] = self._bus.subscribe(topic, self._receive)

    def unsubscribe(self, topic: str) -> None:
        sub_id = self._subs.pop(topic, None)
        if sub_id:
            self._bus.unsubscribe(sub_id)

    def _receive(self, event: Event) -> None:
        self.inbox.put(event)
        if self.on_message:
            try:
                self.on_message(event)
            except Exception:
                pass

    def poll(self, timeout: float = 0.0) -> Optional[Event]:
        try:
            if timeout > 0:
                return self.inbox.get(timeout=timeout)
            return self.inbox.get_nowait()
        except queue.Empty:
            return None

    def drain_all(self) -> List[Event]:
        events = []
        while True:
            ev = self.poll()
            if ev is None:
                break
            events.append(ev)
        return events

    def close(self) -> None:
        for sub_id in self._subs.values():
            self._bus.unsubscribe(sub_id)
        self._subs.clear()


# Topic helpers keep topic naming consistent across services and clients.
def quest_topic(quest_id: str) -> str:
    return f"quest:{quest_id}"


def plan_topic(plan_id: str) -> str:
    return f"skillplan:{plan_id}"


def user_topic(user_id: str) -> str:
    return f"user:{user_id}"


def session_topic(session_id: str) -> str:
    return f"session:{session_id}"


# ======================================================================================
# auth.py
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Authentication & session management. Passwords are stored as PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA256 hashes
# with a per-user random salt. Bearer tokens are random URL-safe strings with an expiry.
# ======================================================================================

import hashlib
import hmac
import os
import re
import secrets


PBKDF2_ITERATIONS = 120_000
TOKEN_TTL_SECONDS = 60 * 60 * 12  # 12 hours
_USERNAME_RE = re.compile(r"^[A-Za-z0-9_]{3,32}$")
_EMAIL_RE = re.compile(r"^[^@\s]+@[^@\s]+\.[^@\s]+$")


def hash_password(password: str, salt: Optional[bytes] = None) -> "tuple[str, str]":
    if salt is None:
        salt = os.urandom(16)
    dk = hashlib.pbkdf2_hmac("sha256", password.encode("utf-8"), salt, PBKDF2_ITERATIONS)
    return salt.hex(), dk.hex()


def verify_password(password: str, salt_hex: str, expected_hex: str) -> bool:
    _, candidate = hash_password(password, bytes.fromhex(salt_hex))
    return hmac.compare_digest(candidate, expected_hex)


class AuthService:
    def __init__(self, users: UserRepository, sessions: AuthSessionRepository,
                 activity: ActivityRepository):
        self.users = users
        self.sessions = sessions
        self.activity = activity

    def register(self, username: str, email: str, password: str,
                 display_name: Optional[str] = None) -> User:
        if not _USERNAME_RE.match(username or ""):
            raise ValidationError("Username must be 3-32 chars: letters, digits, underscore.")
        if not _EMAIL_RE.match(email or ""):
            raise ValidationError("A valid email address is required.")
        if not password or len(password) < 8:
            raise ValidationError("Password must be at least 8 characters.")
        if self.users.by_username(username):
            raise ValidationError(f"Username '{username}' is already taken.")

        salt, pwd_hash = hash_password(password)
        user = User(id=generate_id("usr_"), username=username, email=email,
                    display_name=display_name or username)
        self.users.create(user, salt, pwd_hash)
        self.activity.log(user.id, "user.registered", "user", user.id)
        return user

    def login(self, username: str, password: str) -> "tuple[User, str]":
        row = self.users.by_username(username)
        if not row or not verify_password(password, row["password_salt"], row["password_hash"]):
            raise AuthError("Invalid username or password.")
        token = secrets.token_urlsafe(32)
        self.sessions.create(token, row["id"], TOKEN_TTL_SECONDS)
        user = self.users.by_id(row["id"])
        self.activity.log(user.id, "user.login", "user", user.id)
        return user, token

    def authenticate(self, token: Optional[str]) -> User:
        if not token:
            raise AuthError("Authentication token required.")
        row = self.sessions.get(token)
        if not row:
            raise AuthError("Invalid token.")
        if row["expires_at"] < now_ts():
            self.sessions.delete(token)
            raise AuthError("Token expired.")
        user = self.users.by_id(row["user_id"])
        if not user:
            raise AuthError("User no longer exists.")
        return user

    def logout(self, token: str) -> None:
        self.sessions.delete(token)


# ======================================================================================
# services.py
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Backend business logic. Each service enforces authorization and optimistic concurrency,
# persists through repositories, logs activity, and publishes real-time events so every
# subscribed device stays synchronised. Concurrency control uses a per-resource version
# number; stale writes raise ConflictError (the foundation of safe collaboration).
# ======================================================================================


class QuestService:
    def __init__(self, db: Database, quests: QuestRepository, users: UserRepository,
                 activity: ActivityRepository, bus: EventBus,
                 access_check=None):
        self.db = db
        self.quests = quests
        self.users = users
        self.activity = activity
        self.bus = bus
        # access_check(user_id, "quest", quest_id) -> bool ; injected by CollaborationService
        self._access_check = access_check

    def set_access_check(self, fn) -> None:
        self._access_check = fn

    def _ensure_can_edit(self, user: User, quest: Quest) -> None:
        if quest.owner_id == user.id or quest.assignee_id == user.id:
            return
        if self._access_check and self._access_check(user.id, "quest", quest.id):
            return
        raise PermissionError_("You do not have permission to edit this quest.")

    def _publish(self, event_type: str, quest: Quest, actor_id: str,
                 extra: Dict[str, Any] = None) -> None:
        payload = {"quest": quest.to_dict(), "actor_id": actor_id}
        if extra:
            payload.update(extra)
        self.bus.publish(Event(event_type, quest_topic(quest.id), payload))

    def create_quest(self, user: User, title: str, description: str = "",
                     difficulty: QuestDifficulty = QuestDifficulty.NORMAL,
                     xp_reward: int = 100, objectives: List[str] = None) -> Quest:
        if not title or not title.strip():
            raise ValidationError("Quest title is required.")
        quest = Quest(id=generate_id("qst_"), title=title.strip(), description=description,
                      owner_id=user.id, status=QuestStatus.ACTIVE, difficulty=difficulty,
                      xp_reward=xp_reward)
        for i, desc in enumerate(objectives or []):
            quest.objectives.append(Objective(generate_id("obj_"), quest.id, desc, False, i))
        with self.db.lock:
            self.quests.save(quest)
            for obj in quest.objectives:
                self.quests.save_objective(obj)
        self.activity.log(user.id, "quest.created", "quest", quest.id, {"title": title})
        self._publish("quest.created", quest, user.id)
        return quest

    def get_quest(self, quest_id: str) -> Quest:
        quest = self.quests.by_id(quest_id)
        if not quest:
            raise NotFoundError(f"Quest '{quest_id}' not found.")
        return quest

    def list_quests(self, user: User) -> List[Quest]:
        return self.quests.list_for_user(user.id)

    def update_quest(self, user: User, quest_id: str, changes: Dict[str, Any],
                     expected_version: Optional[int] = None) -> Quest:
        with self.db.lock:
            quest = self.get_quest(quest_id)
            self._ensure_can_edit(user, quest)
            if expected_version is not None and expected_version != quest.version:
                raise ConflictError(
                    f"Version conflict: you edited v{expected_version} but current is "
                    f"v{quest.version}. Refresh and retry.")
            allowed = {"title", "description", "difficulty", "assignee_id",
                       "xp_reward", "status"}
            for key, value in changes.items():
                if key not in allowed:
                    continue
                if key == "difficulty" and not isinstance(value, QuestDifficulty):
                    value = QuestDifficulty(value)
                if key == "status" and not isinstance(value, QuestStatus):
                    value = QuestStatus(value)
                setattr(quest, key, value)
            quest.version += 1
            quest.updated_at = now_ts()
            self.quests.save(quest)
        self.activity.log(user.id, "quest.updated", "quest", quest.id, {"changes": list(changes)})
        self._publish("quest.updated", quest, user.id, {"changes": list(changes.keys())})
        return quest

    def add_objective(self, user: User, quest_id: str, description: str) -> Quest:
        with self.db.lock:
            quest = self.get_quest(quest_id)
            self._ensure_can_edit(user, quest)
            obj = Objective(generate_id("obj_"), quest.id, description, False,
                            len(quest.objectives))
            quest.objectives.append(obj)
            quest.version += 1
            quest.updated_at = now_ts()
            self.quests.save_objective(obj)
            self.quests.save(quest)
        self._publish("quest.objective_added", quest, user.id, {"objective": obj.to_dict()})
        return quest

    def toggle_objective(self, user: User, quest_id: str, objective_id: str,
                         completed: bool) -> Quest:
        with self.db.lock:
            quest = self.get_quest(quest_id)
            self._ensure_can_edit(user, quest)
            target = next((o for o in quest.objectives if o.id == objective_id), None)
            if not target:
                raise NotFoundError(f"Objective '{objective_id}' not found.")
            target.completed = completed
            quest.version += 1
            quest.updated_at = now_ts()
            self.quests.save_objective(target)
            self.quests.save(quest)
        self.activity.log(user.id, "quest.objective_toggled", "quest", quest.id,
                          {"objective_id": objective_id, "completed": completed})
        self._publish("quest.progress", quest, user.id,
                      {"objective_id": objective_id, "completed": completed})
        return quest

    def complete_quest(self, user: User, quest_id: str) -> Quest:
        with self.db.lock:
            quest = self.get_quest(quest_id)
            self._ensure_can_edit(user, quest)
            if quest.status == QuestStatus.COMPLETED:
                raise ValidationError("Quest is already completed.")
            incomplete = [o for o in quest.objectives if not o.completed]
            if incomplete:
                raise ValidationError(
                    f"Cannot complete: {len(incomplete)} objective(s) still open.")
            quest.status = QuestStatus.COMPLETED
            quest.version += 1
            quest.updated_at = now_ts()
            self.quests.save(quest)
            # Award XP to whoever the quest is assigned to (or the owner).
            reward_user = quest.assignee_id or quest.owner_id
            self.users.add_xp(reward_user, quest.xp_reward)
        self.activity.log(user.id, "quest.completed", "quest", quest.id,
                          {"xp_awarded": quest.xp_reward})
        self._publish("quest.completed", quest, user.id, {"xp_awarded": quest.xp_reward})
        return quest

    def delete_quest(self, user: User, quest_id: str) -> None:
        with self.db.lock:
            quest = self.get_quest(quest_id)
            if quest.owner_id != user.id:
                raise PermissionError_("Only the owner can delete a quest.")
            self.quests.delete(quest_id)
        self.activity.log(user.id, "quest.deleted", "quest", quest_id)
        self.bus.publish(Event("quest.deleted", quest_topic(quest_id),
                               {"quest_id": quest_id, "actor_id": user.id}))


class SkillPlanService:
    def __init__(self, db: Database, plans: SkillPlanRepository,
                 activity: ActivityRepository, bus: EventBus, access_check=None):
        self.db = db
        self.plans = plans
        self.activity = activity
        self.bus = bus
        self._access_check = access_check

    def set_access_check(self, fn) -> None:
        self._access_check = fn

    def _ensure_can_edit(self, user: User, plan: SkillPlan) -> None:
        if plan.owner_id == user.id:
            return
        if self._access_check and self._access_check(user.id, "skillplan", plan.id):
            return
        raise PermissionError_("You do not have permission to edit this skill plan.")

    def _publish(self, event_type: str, plan: SkillPlan, actor_id: str,
                 extra: Dict[str, Any] = None) -> None:
        payload = {"plan": plan.to_dict(), "actor_id": actor_id}
        if extra:
            payload.update(extra)
        self.bus.publish(Event(event_type, plan_topic(plan.id), payload))

    def create_plan(self, user: User, name: str, character_name: str,
                    total_points: int = 20, description: str = "") -> SkillPlan:
        if not name or not name.strip():
            raise ValidationError("Skill plan name is required.")
        if total_points <= 0:
            raise ValidationError("total_points must be positive.")
        plan = SkillPlan(id=generate_id("pln_"), name=name.strip(), owner_id=user.id,
                         character_name=character_name, description=description,
                         total_points=total_points)
        self.plans.save(plan)
        self.activity.log(user.id, "skillplan.created", "skillplan", plan.id, {"name": name})
        self._publish("skillplan.created", plan, user.id)
        return plan

    def get_plan(self, plan_id: str) -> SkillPlan:
        plan = self.plans.by_id(plan_id)
        if not plan:
            raise NotFoundError(f"Skill plan '{plan_id}' not found.")
        return plan

    def list_plans(self, user: User) -> List[SkillPlan]:
        return self.plans.list_for_user(user.id)

    def add_skill(self, user: User, plan_id: str, name: str, category: SkillCategory,
                  max_rank: int = 5, cost: int = 1, prerequisites: List[str] = None) -> SkillPlan:
        with self.db.lock:
            plan = self.get_plan(plan_id)
            self._ensure_can_edit(user, plan)
            if not isinstance(category, SkillCategory):
                category = SkillCategory(category)
            skill = Skill(generate_id("skl_"), plan.id, name, category, 0, max_rank, cost,
                          prerequisites or [], len(plan.skills))
            plan.skills.append(skill)
            plan.version += 1
            plan.updated_at = now_ts()
            self.plans.save_skill(skill)
            self.plans.save(plan)
        self._publish("skillplan.skill_added", plan, user.id, {"skill": skill.to_dict()})
        return plan

    def allocate_points(self, user: User, plan_id: str, skill_id: str, new_rank: int,
                        expected_version: Optional[int] = None) -> SkillPlan:
        with self.db.lock:
            plan = self.get_plan(plan_id)
            self._ensure_can_edit(user, plan)
            if expected_version is not None and expected_version != plan.version:
                raise ConflictError(
                    f"Version conflict: edited v{expected_version}, current v{plan.version}.")
            skill = next((s for s in plan.skills if s.id == skill_id), None)
            if not skill:
                raise NotFoundError(f"Skill '{skill_id}' not found.")
            if new_rank < 0 or new_rank > skill.max_rank:
                raise ValidationError(f"Rank must be between 0 and {skill.max_rank}.")
            # Validate prerequisites are at least rank 1 before investing.
            if new_rank > 0:
                for prereq_id in skill.prerequisites:
                    prereq = next((s for s in plan.skills if s.id == prereq_id), None)
                    if prereq and prereq.rank < 1:
                        raise ValidationError(
                            f"Prerequisite '{prereq.name}' must be unlocked first.")
            # Validate the point budget after the change.
            projected = plan.spent_points() - (skill.rank * skill.cost) + (new_rank * skill.cost)
            if projected > plan.total_points:
                raise ValidationError(
                    f"Not enough points: need {projected}, budget is {plan.total_points}.")
            skill.rank = new_rank
            plan.version += 1
            plan.updated_at = now_ts()
            self.plans.save_skill(skill)
            self.plans.save(plan)
        self.activity.log(user.id, "skillplan.allocated", "skillplan", plan.id,
                          {"skill_id": skill_id, "rank": new_rank})
        self._publish("skillplan.allocated", plan, user.id,
                      {"skill_id": skill_id, "rank": new_rank})
        return plan


class CollaborationService:
    """
    Manages real-time collaboration sessions over a quest or skill plan: membership,
    presence, authorization, and live edit fan-out. Edits are applied through the domain
    services (which enforce versioning), so every device subscribed to the resource topic
    receives a synchronised update automatically.
    """

    def __init__(self, db: Database, collab: CollaborationRepository,
                 quests: "QuestService", plans: "SkillPlanService",
                 activity: ActivityRepository, bus: EventBus):
        self.db = db
        self.collab = collab
        self.quests = quests
        self.plans = plans
        self.activity = activity
        self.bus = bus
        # session_id -> {user_id -> RealtimeConnection} : currently connected presence.
        self._presence: Dict[str, Dict[str, RealtimeConnection]] = {}
        self._lock = threading.RLock()
        # Wire authorization back into the domain services.
        quests.set_access_check(self.has_edit_access)
        plans.set_access_check(self.has_edit_access)

    # -- authorization -----------------------------------------------------------------
    def has_edit_access(self, user_id: str, resource_type: str, resource_id: str) -> bool:
        session = self.collab.find_for_resource(resource_type, resource_id)
        if not session or not session.active:
            return False
        role = session.participants.get(user_id)
        return role in (CollaboratorRole.OWNER, CollaboratorRole.EDITOR)

    # -- session lifecycle -------------------------------------------------------------
    def _topic_for(self, resource_type: str, resource_id: str) -> str:
        return quest_topic(resource_id) if resource_type == "quest" else plan_topic(resource_id)

    def start_session(self, owner: User, resource_type: str, resource_id: str
                      ) -> CollaborationSession:
        if resource_type not in ("quest", "skillplan"):
            raise ValidationError("resource_type must be 'quest' or 'skillplan'.")
        # Validate the resource exists and the owner controls it.
        if resource_type == "quest":
            resource = self.quests.get_quest(resource_id)
        else:
            resource = self.plans.get_plan(resource_id)
        if resource.owner_id != owner.id:
            raise PermissionError_("Only the resource owner can start a collaboration session.")
        existing = self.collab.find_for_resource(resource_type, resource_id)
        if existing:
            return existing
        session = CollaborationSession(generate_id("col_"), resource_type, resource_id, owner.id)
        session.participants[owner.id] = CollaboratorRole.OWNER
        with self.db.lock:
            self.collab.save_session(session)
            self.collab.add_participant(session.id, owner.id, CollaboratorRole.OWNER)
        self.activity.log(owner.id, "collab.started", resource_type, resource_id,
                          {"session_id": session.id})
        return session

    def invite(self, owner: User, session_id: str, user_id: str,
               role: CollaboratorRole = CollaboratorRole.EDITOR) -> CollaborationSession:
        session = self._get_session(session_id)
        if session.owner_id != owner.id:
            raise PermissionError_("Only the session owner can invite collaborators.")
        with self.db.lock:
            self.collab.add_participant(session_id, user_id, role)
        session = self._get_session(session_id)
        # Notify the invited user on their personal channel.
        self.bus.publish(Event("collab.invited", user_topic(user_id),
                               {"session_id": session_id, "resource_type": session.resource_type,
                                "resource_id": session.resource_id, "role": role.value}))
        self.activity.log(owner.id, "collab.invited", session.resource_type,
                          session.resource_id, {"user_id": user_id, "role": role.value})
        return session

    def join(self, user: User, session_id: str, connection: RealtimeConnection
             ) -> CollaborationSession:
        session = self._get_session(session_id)
        if user.id not in session.participants:
            raise PermissionError_("You have not been invited to this session.")
        topic = self._topic_for(session.resource_type, session.resource_id)
        connection.subscribe(topic)
        connection.subscribe(session_topic(session_id))
        with self._lock:
            self._presence.setdefault(session_id, {})[user.id] = connection
        # Announce presence to everyone in the session.
        self.bus.publish(Event("presence.join", session_topic(session_id),
                               {"user_id": user.id, "display_name": user.display_name,
                                "online": self.online_users(session_id)}))
        return session

    def leave(self, user: User, session_id: str, connection: RealtimeConnection) -> None:
        session = self.collab.by_id(session_id)
        if session:
            topic = self._topic_for(session.resource_type, session.resource_id)
            connection.unsubscribe(topic)
            connection.unsubscribe(session_topic(session_id))
        with self._lock:
            conns = self._presence.get(session_id, {})
            conns.pop(user.id, None)
        self.bus.publish(Event("presence.leave", session_topic(session_id),
                               {"user_id": user.id, "online": self.online_users(session_id)}))

    def online_users(self, session_id: str) -> List[str]:
        with self._lock:
            return list(self._presence.get(session_id, {}).keys())

    def broadcast_chat(self, user: User, session_id: str, message: str) -> None:
        self._get_session(session_id)  # validates existence
        self.bus.publish(Event("collab.chat", session_topic(session_id),
                               {"user_id": user.id, "display_name": user.display_name,
                                "message": message}))

    def _get_session(self, session_id: str) -> CollaborationSession:
        session = self.collab.by_id(session_id)
        if not session:
            raise NotFoundError(f"Collaboration session '{session_id}' not found.")
        return session

# API surface. Both the HTTP server and the programmatic frontend client talk to this.

class QuestHub:
    def __init__(self, db_path: str = ":memory:"):
        self.db = Database(db_path)
        self.bus = EventBus()

        # Repositories
        self.users_repo = UserRepository(self.db)
        self.sessions_repo = AuthSessionRepository(self.db)
        self.quests_repo = QuestRepository(self.db)
        self.plans_repo = SkillPlanRepository(self.db)
        self.collab_repo = CollaborationRepository(self.db)
        self.activity_repo = ActivityRepository(self.db)

        # Services
        self.auth = AuthService(self.users_repo, self.sessions_repo, self.activity_repo)
        self.quests = QuestService(self.db, self.quests_repo, self.users_repo,
                                   self.activity_repo, self.bus)
        self.plans = SkillPlanService(self.db, self.plans_repo, self.activity_repo, self.bus)
        self.collab = CollaborationService(self.db, self.collab_repo, self.quests,
                                           self.plans, self.activity_repo, self.bus)

    def open_connection(self, user: User, on_message=None) -> RealtimeConnection:
        """Open a real-time connection for a user and auto-subscribe to their channel."""
        conn = RealtimeConnection(user.id, self.bus, on_message=on_message)
        conn.subscribe(user_topic(user.id))
        return conn

    def shutdown(self) -> None:
        self.bus.stop()
        self.db.close()


# ======================================================================================
# api.py
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# An HTTP REST API exposing the application over the network, plus a frontend client.
# The API is a thin translation layer: parse request -> call facade -> serialise result,
# mapping typed exceptions onto HTTP status codes.
# ======================================================================================

from http.server import BaseHTTPRequestHandler, ThreadingHTTPServer
from urllib.parse import urlparse


class _Router:
    """Tiny regex router mapping (METHOD, path-pattern) to handler callables."""

    def __init__(self):
        self.routes = []

    def add(self, method: str, pattern: str, handler) -> None:
        regex = re.compile("^" + re.sub(r"\{(\w+)\}", r"(?P<\1>[^/]+)", pattern) + "$")
        self.routes.append((method.upper(), regex, handler))

    def match(self, method: str, path: str):
        for m, regex, handler in self.routes:
            if m != method.upper():
                continue
            match = regex.match(path)
            if match:
                return handler, match.groupdict()
        return None, None


def build_api(hub: QuestHub) -> "_Router":
    router = _Router()

    def register(user, body, params):
        u = hub.auth.register(body.get("username"), body.get("email"),
                              body.get("password"), body.get("display_name"))
        return 201, u.to_dict()

    def login(user, body, params):
        u, token = hub.auth.login(body.get("username"), body.get("password"))
        return 200, {"user": u.to_dict(), "token": token}

    def list_quests(user, body, params):
        return 200, {"quests": [q.to_dict() for q in hub.quests.list_quests(user)]}

    def create_quest(user, body, params):
        diff = QuestDifficulty(body.get("difficulty", 3))
        q = hub.quests.create_quest(user, body.get("title", ""), body.get("description", ""),
                                    diff, body.get("xp_reward", 100), body.get("objectives"))
        return 201, q.to_dict()

    def get_quest(user, body, params):
        return 200, hub.quests.get_quest(params["id"]).to_dict()

    def update_quest(user, body, params):
        q = hub.quests.update_quest(user, params["id"], body.get("changes", {}),
                                    body.get("expected_version"))
        return 200, q.to_dict()

    def complete_quest(user, body, params):
        return 200, hub.quests.complete_quest(user, params["id"]).to_dict()

    def create_plan(user, body, params):
        p = hub.plans.create_plan(user, body.get("name", ""), body.get("character_name", ""),
                                  body.get("total_points", 20), body.get("description", ""))
        return 201, p.to_dict()

    def get_plan(user, body, params):
        return 200, hub.plans.get_plan(params["id"]).to_dict()

    def add_skill(user, body, params):
        p = hub.plans.add_skill(user, params["id"], body.get("name", ""),
                                SkillCategory(body.get("category", "combat")),
                                body.get("max_rank", 5), body.get("cost", 1),
                                body.get("prerequisites"))
        return 201, p.to_dict()

    def start_collab(user, body, params):
        s = hub.collab.start_session(user, body.get("resource_type"), body.get("resource_id"))
        return 201, s.to_dict()

    def invite_collab(user, body, params):
        s = hub.collab.invite(user, params["id"], body.get("user_id"),
                              CollaboratorRole(body.get("role", "editor")))
        return 200, s.to_dict()

    def health(user, body, params):
        return 200, {"status": "ok", "service": "QuestHub"}

    # Public routes (no auth) and protected routes (auth required) are split by the handler.
    router.add("GET", "/api/health", health)
    router.add("POST", "/api/register", register)
    router.add("POST", "/api/login", login)
    router.add("GET", "/api/quests", list_quests)
    router.add("POST", "/api/quests", create_quest)
    router.add("GET", "/api/quests/{id}", get_quest)
    router.add("PATCH", "/api/quests/{id}", update_quest)
    router.add("POST", "/api/quests/{id}/complete", complete_quest)
    router.add("POST", "/api/skillplans", create_plan)
    router.add("GET", "/api/skillplans/{id}", get_plan)
    router.add("POST", "/api/skillplans/{id}/skills", add_skill)
    router.add("POST", "/api/collab/sessions", start_collab)
    router.add("POST", "/api/collab/sessions/{id}/invite", invite_collab)
    return router


# Routes that do not require an Authorization header.
_PUBLIC_ROUTES = {("GET", "/api/health"), ("POST", "/api/register"), ("POST", "/api/login")}


def make_request_handler(hub: QuestHub, router: "_Router"):
    class Handler(BaseHTTPRequestHandler):
        protocol_version = "HTTP/1.1"

        def log_message(self, *args):  # silence default stderr logging
            pass

        def _send(self, status: int, body: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:
            data = json.dumps(body).encode("utf-8")
            self.send_response(status)
            self.send_header("Content-Type", "application/json")
            self.send_header("Content-Length", str(len(data)))
            self.end_headers()
            self.wfile.write(data)

        def _read_body(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
            length = int(self.headers.get("Content-Length", 0) or 0)
            if length == 0:
                return {}
            raw = self.rfile.read(length)
            try:
                return json.loads(raw.decode("utf-8")) if raw else {}
            except json.JSONDecodeError:
                raise ValidationError("Request body must be valid JSON.")

        def _bearer_token(self) -> Optional[str]:
            header = self.headers.get("Authorization", "")
            if header.startswith("Bearer "):
                return header[len("Bearer "):].strip()
            return None

        def _dispatch(self, method: str) -> None:
            path = urlparse(self.path).path
            handler, params = router.match(method, path)
            if handler is None:
                self._send(404, {"error": "Not found", "path": path})
                return
            try:
                body = self._read_body() if method in ("POST", "PATCH", "PUT") else {}
                user = None
                # Determine if this concrete route requires authentication.
                route_key = (method, path)
                is_public = route_key in _PUBLIC_ROUTES
                if not is_public:
                    user = hub.auth.authenticate(self._bearer_token())
                status, payload = handler(user, body, params)
                self._send(status, payload)
            except QuestHubError as exc:
                self._send(exc.status, {"error": str(exc), "type": type(exc).__name__})
            except Exception as exc:  # pragma: no cover - defensive
                self._send(500, {"error": "Internal server error", "detail": str(exc)})

        def do_GET(self):
            self._dispatch("GET")

        def do_POST(self):
            self._dispatch("POST")

        def do_PATCH(self):
            self._dispatch("PATCH")

    return Handler


class ApiServer:
    """Wraps a threaded HTTP server with start/stop lifecycle management."""

    def __init__(self, hub: QuestHub, host: str = "127.0.0.1", port: int = 0):
        self.hub = hub
        router = build_api(hub)
        handler = make_request_handler(hub, router)
        self._server = ThreadingHTTPServer((host, port), handler)
        self._thread: Optional[threading.Thread] = None

    @property
    def port(self) -> int:
        return self._server.server_address[1]

    @property
    def base_url(self) -> str:
        host, port = self._server.server_address
        return f"http://{host}:{port}"

    def start(self) -> None:
        self._thread = threading.Thread(target=self._server.serve_forever, daemon=True)
        self._thread.start()

    def stop(self) -> None:
        self._server.shutdown()
        self._server.server_close()
        if self._thread:
            self._thread.join(timeout=2)


# --- Frontend client -------------------------------------------------------------------
# QuestHubClient is the "frontend": a thin client that calls the application (in-process)
# and maintains a live real-time connection so the UI can react to collaboration events.


class QuestHubClient:
    def __init__(self, hub: QuestHub):
        self.hub = hub
        self.user: Optional[User] = None
        self.token: Optional[str] = None
        self.connection: Optional[RealtimeConnection] = None
        self.received_events: List[Event] = []

    # -- session ----------------------------------------------------------------------
    def register(self, username: str, email: str, password: str,
                 display_name: Optional[str] = None) -> User:
        return self.hub.auth.register(username, email, password, display_name)

    def login(self, username: str, password: str) -> User:
        self.user, self.token = self.hub.auth.login(username, password)
        self.connection = self.hub.open_connection(self.user, on_message=self._on_event)
        return self.user

    def _on_event(self, event: Event) -> None:
        # The frontend reacts here: in a GUI this would re-render; we just record it.
        self.received_events.append(event)

    def logout(self) -> None:
        if self.token:
            self.hub.auth.logout(self.token)
        if self.connection:
            self.connection.close()
        self.user = self.token = self.connection = None

    # -- quests -----------------------------------------------------------------------
    def create_quest(self, title: str, **kwargs) -> Quest:
        return self.hub.quests.create_quest(self.user, title, **kwargs)

    def open_quest(self, quest_id: str) -> Quest:
        # Opening a resource subscribes the client's connection to its live topic.
        if self.connection:
            self.connection.subscribe(quest_topic(quest_id))
        return self.hub.quests.get_quest(quest_id)

    def update_quest(self, quest_id: str, changes: Dict[str, Any],
                     expected_version: Optional[int] = None) -> Quest:
        return self.hub.quests.update_quest(self.user, quest_id, changes, expected_version)

    def toggle_objective(self, quest_id: str, objective_id: str, completed: bool) -> Quest:
        return self.hub.quests.toggle_objective(self.user, quest_id, objective_id, completed)

    def complete_quest(self, quest_id: str) -> Quest:
        return self.hub.quests.complete_quest(self.user, quest_id)

    def list_quests(self) -> List[Quest]:
        return self.hub.quests.list_quests(self.user)

    # -- skill plans ------------------------------------------------------------------
    def create_plan(self, name: str, character_name: str, **kwargs) -> SkillPlan:
        return self.hub.plans.create_plan(self.user, name, character_name, **kwargs)

    def open_plan(self, plan_id: str) -> SkillPlan:
        if self.connection:
            self.connection.subscribe(plan_topic(plan_id))
        return self.hub.plans.get_plan(plan_id)

    def add_skill(self, plan_id: str, name: str, category: SkillCategory, **kwargs) -> SkillPlan:
        return self.hub.plans.add_skill(self.user, plan_id, name, category, **kwargs)

    def allocate_points(self, plan_id: str, skill_id: str, rank: int,
                        expected_version: Optional[int] = None) -> SkillPlan:
        return self.hub.plans.allocate_points(self.user, plan_id, skill_id, rank,
                                              expected_version)

    # -- collaboration ----------------------------------------------------------------
    def start_collaboration(self, resource_type: str, resource_id: str
                            ) -> CollaborationSession:
        return self.hub.collab.start_session(self.user, resource_type, resource_id)

    def invite(self, session_id: str, user_id: str,
               role: CollaboratorRole = CollaboratorRole.EDITOR) -> CollaborationSession:
        return self.hub.collab.invite(self.user, session_id, user_id, role)

    def join_collaboration(self, session_id: str) -> CollaborationSession:
        return self.hub.collab.join(self.user, session_id, self.connection)

    def send_chat(self, session_id: str, message: str) -> None:
        self.hub.collab.broadcast_chat(self.user, session_id, message)

    def pending_events(self) -> List[Event]:
        return list(self.received_events)
