import sys
import urllib.request
import urllib.error


def _http_json(method: str, url: str, body: Dict[str, Any] = None,
               token: str = None) -> "tuple[int, Dict[str, Any]]":
    data = json.dumps(body).encode("utf-8") if body is not None else None
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, data=data, method=method)
    req.add_header("Content-Type", "application/json")
    if token:
        req.add_header("Authorization", f"Bearer {token}")
    try:
        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=5) as resp:
            return resp.status, json.loads(resp.read().decode("utf-8"))
    except urllib.error.HTTPError as exc:
        return exc.code, json.loads(exc.read().decode("utf-8"))


def run_self_tests() -> int:
    """Execute an assertion-based test suite covering every subsystem. Returns test count."""
    print("Running QuestHub self-test suite...")
    tests = 0
    hub = QuestHub()
    try:
        # --- Authentication ----------------------------------------------------------
        alice = hub.auth.register("alice", "alice@example.com", "password123", "Alice")
        assert alice.id.startswith("usr_"); tests += 1
        try:
            hub.auth.register("alice", "dup@example.com", "password123")
            assert False, "duplicate username should fail"
        except ValidationError:
            tests += 1
        try:
            hub.auth.register("bo", "bad", "short")
            assert False, "invalid input should fail"
        except ValidationError:
            tests += 1

        user_a, token_a = hub.auth.login("alice", "password123")
        assert hub.auth.authenticate(token_a).id == alice.id; tests += 1
        try:
            hub.auth.login("alice", "wrongpassword")
            assert False
        except AuthError:
            tests += 1

        bob = hub.auth.register("bob", "bob@example.com", "password123", "Bob")
        user_b, token_b = hub.auth.login("bob", "password123")

        # --- Quest management --------------------------------------------------------
        quest = hub.quests.create_quest(user_a, "Slay the Dragon", "Defeat Vermithrax",
                                        QuestDifficulty.EPIC, 500,
                                        ["Find the lair", "Forge a sword", "Win the fight"])
        assert quest.status == QuestStatus.ACTIVE and len(quest.objectives) == 3; tests += 1
        assert abs(quest.progress()) < 1e-9; tests += 1

        # Cannot complete with open objectives.
        try:
            hub.quests.complete_quest(user_a, quest.id)
            assert False
        except ValidationError:
            tests += 1

        for obj in quest.objectives:
            hub.quests.toggle_objective(user_a, quest.id, obj.id, True)
        done = hub.quests.complete_quest(user_a, quest.id)
        assert done.status == QuestStatus.COMPLETED; tests += 1
        assert hub.users_repo.by_id(user_a.id).xp == 500; tests += 1

        # Optimistic concurrency: stale version is rejected.
        q2 = hub.quests.create_quest(user_a, "Gather Herbs")
        hub.quests.update_quest(user_a, q2.id, {"description": "first"})
        try:
            hub.quests.update_quest(user_a, q2.id, {"description": "stale"}, expected_version=1)
            assert False
        except ConflictError:
            tests += 1

        # Authorization: Bob cannot edit Alice's quest without collaboration.
        try:
            hub.quests.update_quest(user_b, q2.id, {"title": "hijack"})
            assert False
        except PermissionError_:
            tests += 1

        # --- Skill planning ----------------------------------------------------------
        plan = hub.plans.create_plan(user_a, "Battlemage Build", "Aldric", total_points=10)
        plan = hub.plans.add_skill(user_a, plan.id, "Fireball", SkillCategory.MAGIC,
                                   max_rank=5, cost=2)
        fireball = plan.skills[0]
        plan = hub.plans.add_skill(user_a, plan.id, "Inferno", SkillCategory.MAGIC,
                                   max_rank=3, cost=2, prerequisites=[fireball.id])
        inferno = plan.skills[1]

        # Prerequisite enforcement: cannot rank Inferno before Fireball.
        try:
            hub.plans.allocate_points(user_a, plan.id, inferno.id, 1)
            assert False
        except ValidationError:
            tests += 1

        plan = hub.plans.allocate_points(user_a, plan.id, fireball.id, 3)  # 3*2 = 6 points
        assert plan.spent_points() == 6 and plan.remaining_points() == 4; tests += 1

        # Budget enforcement: 2 ranks of Inferno (cost 2) = 4 -> total 10 ok, 3 ranks too many.
        try:
            hub.plans.allocate_points(user_a, plan.id, inferno.id, 3)  # would be 6+6=12 > 10
            assert False
        except ValidationError:
            tests += 1
        plan = hub.plans.allocate_points(user_a, plan.id, inferno.id, 2)
        assert plan.spent_points() == 10; tests += 1

        # --- Real-time collaboration -------------------------------------------------
        conn_a = hub.open_connection(user_a)
        conn_b = hub.open_connection(user_b)

        collab_quest = hub.quests.create_quest(user_a, "Co-op Raid", "Team up", xp_reward=300)
        session = hub.collab.start_session(user_a, "quest", collab_quest.id)
        hub.collab.invite(user_a, session.id, user_b.id, CollaboratorRole.EDITOR)

        hub.collab.join(user_a, session.id, conn_a)
        hub.collab.join(user_b, session.id, conn_b)
        assert set(hub.collab.online_users(session.id)) == {user_a.id, user_b.id}; tests += 1

        # Now Bob (an invited editor) CAN edit the quest.
        hub.quests.update_quest(user_b, collab_quest.id, {"description": "Bob's plan"})
        hub.bus.drain()

        # Both connections received the live update on the quest topic.
        events_a = conn_a.drain_all()
        events_b = conn_b.drain_all()
        assert any(e.type == "quest.updated" for e in events_a); tests += 1
        assert any(e.type == "quest.updated" for e in events_b); tests += 1
        # Bob also received the personal invite + presence events earlier.
        assert any(e.type == "presence.join" for e in events_a + events_b); tests += 1

        # Chat fan-out.
        hub.collab.broadcast_chat(user_a, session.id, "Ready to raid?")
        hub.bus.drain()
        assert any(e.type == "collab.chat" for e in conn_b.drain_all()); tests += 1

        conn_a.close(); conn_b.close()

        # --- HTTP REST API (integration) ---------------------------------------------
        api_ok = True
        try:
            server = ApiServer(hub, port=0)
            server.start()
            base = server.base_url
            code, health = _http_json("GET", f"{base}/api/health")
            assert code == 200 and health["status"] == "ok"; tests += 1
            code, body = _http_json("POST", f"{base}/api/login",
                                    {"username": "alice", "password": "password123"})
            assert code == 200 and "token" in body; tests += 1
            api_token = body["token"]
            code, created = _http_json("POST", f"{base}/api/quests",
                                       {"title": "API Quest", "difficulty": 2}, token=api_token)
            assert code == 201 and created["title"] == "API Quest"; tests += 1
            # Unauthorized request is rejected.
            code, _ = _http_json("GET", f"{base}/api/quests")
            assert code == 401; tests += 1
            server.stop()
        except OSError:
            # Networking may be restricted in some sandboxes; the in-process path still works.
            api_ok = False
            print("  (HTTP API test skipped: sockets unavailable in this environment)")

        print(f"All {tests} assertions passed."
              + ("" if api_ok else " [HTTP subset skipped]"))
        return tests
    finally:
        hub.shutdown()


def run_demo() -> None:
    """A short narrative walk-through printed to stdout."""
    print("\n" + "=" * 70)
    print("QuestHub - live demonstration")
    print("=" * 70)
    hub = QuestHub()
    try:
        # Two players register and log in via the frontend client.
        thalia = QuestHubClient(hub)
        thalia.register("thalia", "thalia@questhub.gg", "dragonslayer", "Thalia")
        thalia.login("thalia", "dragonslayer")

        garrick = QuestHubClient(hub)
        garrick.register("garrick", "garrick@questhub.gg", "shieldwall", "Garrick")
        garrick.login("garrick", "shieldwall")
        print(f"\n[auth] {thalia.user.display_name} and {garrick.user.display_name} logged in.")

        # Thalia creates a quest with objectives.
        quest = thalia.create_quest(
            "The Sunken Crypt",
            description="Clear the flooded crypt beneath Eldermoor.",
            difficulty=QuestDifficulty.HARD, xp_reward=350,
            objectives=["Drain the chambers", "Defeat the Drowned King", "Recover the relic"])
        print(f"\n[quest] '{quest.title}' created with {len(quest.objectives)} objectives "
              f"(difficulty={quest.difficulty.name}, reward={quest.xp_reward} XP).")

        # Thalia starts a collaboration session and invites Garrick.
        session = thalia.start_collaboration("quest", quest.id)
        thalia.invite(session.id, garrick.user.id, CollaboratorRole.EDITOR)
        thalia.join_collaboration(session.id)
        garrick.open_quest(quest.id)
        garrick.join_collaboration(session.id)
        print(f"[collab] Session {session.id[:14]}... started; "
              f"online: {hub.collab.online_users(session.id)}")

        # Garrick (editor) completes an objective; Thalia's client gets the live update.
        target = quest.objectives[0]
        garrick.toggle_objective(quest.id, target.id, True)
        hub.bus.drain()
        live = [e.type for e in thalia.pending_events()]
        print(f"[realtime] Garrick checked off '{target.description}'. "
              f"Thalia's client received events: {sorted(set(live))}")

        # They chat in real time.
        thalia.send_chat(session.id, "Nice! I'll drain the next chamber.")
        hub.bus.drain()
        chats = [e.payload["message"] for e in garrick.pending_events()
                 if e.type == "collab.chat"]
        print(f"[chat] Garrick sees: {chats}")

        # Thalia builds a skill plan and allocates points.
        plan = thalia.create_plan("Tempest Cleric", "Thalia", total_points=12,
                                  description="Storm-focused support build.")
        plan = thalia.add_skill(plan.id, "Chain Lightning", SkillCategory.MAGIC, cost=3)
        plan = thalia.add_skill(plan.id, "Healing Rain", SkillCategory.MAGIC, cost=2)
        plan = thalia.allocate_points(plan.id, plan.skills[0].id, 2)
        plan = thalia.allocate_points(plan.id, plan.skills[1].id, 3)
        print(f"\n[skills] Plan '{plan.name}': spent {plan.spent_points()}/"
              f"{plan.total_points} points across {len(plan.skills)} skills.")

        # Finish remaining objectives and complete the quest -> XP awarded.
        for obj in quest.objectives[1:]:
            garrick.toggle_objective(quest.id, obj.id, True)
        completed = thalia.complete_quest(quest.id)
        hub.bus.drain()
        thalia_xp = hub.users_repo.by_id(thalia.user.id).xp
        print(f"\n[quest] '{completed.title}' completed! Status={completed.status.value}, "
              f"Thalia now has {thalia_xp} XP (level {hub.users_repo.by_id(thalia.user.id).level}).")

        # Activity feed demonstrates the audit/history capability.
        feed = hub.activity_repo.recent("quest", quest.id, limit=5)
        print("\n[activity] Most recent quest events:")
        for entry in feed:
            print(f"    - {entry['action']} by {entry['actor_id'][:12]}... at {entry['at']}")

        print("\nDemo complete. QuestHub integrated auth, quests, skills, "
              "and real-time collaboration end to end.")
    finally:
        thalia.logout()
        garrick.logout()
        hub.shutdown()


def serve(host: str = "127.0.0.1", port: int = 8080) -> None:
    """Run the HTTP API server until interrupted."""
    hub = QuestHub()
    server = ApiServer(hub, host=host, port=port)
    server.start()
    print(f"QuestHub API listening on {server.base_url}")
    print("Try:  curl -s {0}/api/health".format(server.base_url))
    print("Press Ctrl+C to stop.")
    try:
        while True:
            time.sleep(1)
    except KeyboardInterrupt:
        print("\nShutting down...")
    finally:
        server.stop()
        hub.shutdown()


def main(argv: List[str]) -> int:
    args = set(argv[1:])
    if "--serve" in args:
        serve()
        return 0
    if "--demo" in args and "--test" not in args:
        run_demo()
        return 0
    if "--test" in args and "--demo" not in args:
        run_self_tests()
        return 0
    # Default: run tests, then the demo.
    run_self_tests()
    run_demo()
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(main(sys.argv))
